description_long: This kiosk is constructed entirely from white marble and features a classical architectural design reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman monuments. The structure is rectangular with a flat, overhanging roof supported by four fluted Ionic columns at the front and rear, each topped with ornate capitals. The sides are solid marble panels, while the front and back are open, allowing passage through the central space. The base consists of several broad, stepped marble platforms, giving the kiosk a monumental and elevated appearance. Subtle weathering and surface texture variations are visible on the marble, adding realism. The only signage is a small, engraved text reading 'KENNY PARK' on the front step.
description: A white marble kiosk with classical columns, open front and back, and a stepped base, styled like an ancient monument. Subtle signage is engraved on the front step.
description_view_0: Front view showing four Ionic columns, open central passage, and 'KENNY PARK' signage engraved on the front step.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a solid marble panel with no openings or signage, and two columns partially visible at the corners.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with four columns and an open passage, but no visible signage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to view 1, with a solid marble panel and no openings or signage.
attribute_signage_type: engraved sign on the front step with the text 'KENNY PARK'
attribute_door_configuration: open archway at front and rear, no doors
attribute_roof_design: flat, overhanging marble roof with clean edges and classical cornice detailing
attribute_architectural_style: classical Greek or Roman revival with Ionic columns and monumental symmetry
